ksa_text: Knowledge of AI security threats and adversarial ML techniques, including data poisoning, model extraction, evasion attacks, prompt injection, and the defensive measures and robustness testing approaches applicable to each.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of Extensible Markup Language (XML) schemas

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of Extensible Markup Language (XML) schemas

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain AI/ML Engineering KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.397) to justify inclusion for the Director of Data Engineering. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world Director of Data Engineering roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm AI/ML Engineering is not a standard competency expectation for Director of Data Engineering. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the Director of Data Engineering competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.397) and edge-domain placement (AI/ML Engineering) do not meet inclusion threshold for the Director of Data Engineering. All passes support exclusion.
